scores.correctness_notes: Gets the core concepts right (deduplication, custom type, change-update, pebble_custom_notice event). However, the gold standard specifies 3 types (change-update, custom, warning) — the agent omits 'warning' and adds two types not in the gold standard (check-failed, check-recovered). The deduplication identity is given as (type, key) but the gold standard says (user ID, type, key). Also omits that notices persist across restarts and expire after a defined interval.
scores.hallucination_notes: check-failed and check-recovered notice types are not in the gold standard and may not exist at the scope described. Corresponding ops event objects (PebbleCheckFailedEvent, PebbleCheckRecoveredEvent) and event.info.name API are unverified against the gold standard. Uses ops.pebble.Notice.CUSTOM constant naming — the correct enum is ops.pebble.NoticeType.CUSTOM. Missing user ID in the deduplication key is a factual error rather than hallucination per se.
